scope.summary: Derive a checked machine-readable CI job taxonomy (required/advisory/scheduled/release/manual) from the workflow YAML, and classify a SHA's check-runs without ever calling CI green while a required job is not a completed success.
in_scope: docs/ci_taxonomy.json (25 jobs: 11 required, 2 advisory, 6 scheduled, 4 release, 2 manual)
Cross-repo shape reconciliation with the oraclemcp mirror (yg4x.8)
out_of_scope: Wiring the drift gate into a CI workflow: no workflow YAML was changed, only read.
Emitting release-candidate-proof/v1 from --status output; f1cl.3 consumes this shape and owns that.
Branch-protection configuration on GitHub: the taxonomy describes what the YAML says gates, and does not reconcile it against branch-protection settings.

entry_points.name: scripts/check_ci_taxonomy.sh
entry_points.trace: Offline drift gate: exits non-zero when a job appears, disappears, or changes tier. Negative control detected 4/4 planted drift scenarios, including a required job gaining continue-on-error.

entry_points.name: scripts/ci_taxonomy.py --status <SHA>
entry_points.trace: Classifies live check-runs; at ea7a3c6 returned ci_green=false attributing 4 path-filtered version-matrix lanes, exit 1 with empty stderr and valid JSON on stdout.

entry_points.name: scripts/ci_taxonomy.py --verify-names <SHA>
entry_points.trace: Asserts every real check-run is classified; all 9 real check-runs at ea7a3c6 matched, 0 unknown. This is what proves the YAML derivation matches what GitHub actually publishes.

limitations: readiness.basis is scoped-test, so this close cannot claim ready: the Required graph (clippy, workspace test) was NOT run. The diff contains zero Rust and the shared checkout held another pane's in-flight Rust edits. Only cargo fmt -p oracledb --check was run, deliberately scoped rather than --all so it would not reformat another pane's WIP.

source.tree_clean asserts that every file this close claims is committed at source.sha, verified with `git status --porcelain -- <scope.in_scope>`. It is NOT a claim that the whole working directory was pristine: this is a shared checkout and it carried other panes' uncommitted Rust at the time. See docs/BEAD_CLOSE_EVIDENCE.md; a required-proof, whose commands actually execute against the working tree, uses the stricter whole-tree reading and none is referenced here.
--status and --verify-names query the GitHub check-runs API and therefore need network and gh auth; only --list/--write/--check are offline. The drift gate that runs in CI is the offline one.

Tiers are derived from workflow triggers and continue-on-error, not from GitHub branch-protection settings. A job the YAML says is required but that branch protection does not enforce would not be flagged.
--verify-names is one-directional: an unknown check-run fails, but a derived job with no check-run is accepted, because a path filter or schedule legitimately means it never ran.
